title: Audio drill task
description: A task is an ordered list of lines. tts.py renders each line to a TTS clip, adds `gap` seconds of silence after it, then stitches everything into one .mp4 (audio + soft subtitles) plus a .srt. Each line's text also becomes one subtitle cue. Global defaults (the voice per language, etc.) live in tts.py and can be overridden per line.

lines.description: The lines to speak, in order.

lang.description: Language code. Selects the default voice + provider locale from tts.py VOICES.

text.description: The text to speak. Also shown as this line's subtitle cue.

voice.description: Override the voice for this line. Use 'f' or 'm' as a shorthand for the female/male voice of this language, or give a full provider voice id. Defaults to VOICES[lang] in tts.py.

provider.description: Which TTS service renders this line. Only 'google' is implemented today; 'azure'/'elevenlabs' are reserved for languages Google can't speak (e.g. Persian).

speed.description: Speaking rate. <1 = slower, >1 = faster. For a slow repeat, add the same line again with a lower speed.

gap.description: Seconds of silence after this line, before the next one.
